LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANNA MAU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UNRUH ACT, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") ADEQUATELY PLEADS ALL OF THE REQUISITE ELEMENTS OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION - (1) THAT DEFENDANT DENIED FULL AND EQUAL SERVICES TO THE PLAINTIFF; (2) THAT A SUBSTANTIAL MOTIVATING REASON FOR DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT WAS ITS PERCEPTION OF PLAINTIFF'S RACE; (3) THAT PLAINTIFF WAS HARMED; AND (4) THAT DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING PLAINTIFF'S HARM. (CACI 3060; FAC, PARS. 24-25, 27, 37-39.) THE FAC ADEQUATELY PLEADS FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE STANDING, AS THE FAC ALLEGES THAT THEY WERE DENIED SERVICES DUE TO THEIR RACE. CALIFORNIA CASES HAVE "ALLOWED PARTIES THAT ARE NOT CLIENTS, PATRONS, OR CUSTOMERS IN THE TRADITIONAL SENSE TO BRING UNRUH ACT CLAIMS." (STROTHER V. SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP (9TH CIR. 1996) 79 F.3D 859, 873; SEE E.G. JACKSON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1994) 30 CAL.APP.4TH 936.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING "CONDUCT SO EXTREME AS TO EXCEED ALL BOUNDS OF THAT USUALLY TOLERATED IN A CIVILIZED COMMUNITY." (COCHRAN V. COCHRAN (1998) 65 CAL.APP.4TH 488, 494.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SZE-HANG LO OF SHL LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 831-3388 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN C. HARRIMAN OF CODDINGTON HICKS & DANFORTH, TEL. NO. (650)592-5400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the second cause of action for Violation of the Unruh Act, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads all of the requisite elements of the cause of action - (1) that defendant denied full and equal services to the plaintiff; (2) that a substantial motivating reason for defendant's conduct was its perception of plaintiff's race; (3) that plaintiff was harmed; and (4) that defendant's conduct was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff's harm. (CACI 3060; FAC, pars. 24-25, 27, 37-39.) The FAC adequately pleads facts establishing that Plaintiffs have standing, as the FAC alleges that they were denied services due to their race. California cases have "allowed parties that are not clients, patrons, or customers in the traditional sense to bring Unruh Act claims." (Strother v. Southern California Permanente Medical Group (9th Cir. 1996) 79 F.3d 859, 873; see e.g. Jackson v. Superior Court (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 936.) As to the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The FAC does not plead facts establishing "conduct so extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in a civilized community." (Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 488, 494.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
